label: CurbClass Leads
info: Endpoint webhooks and Gotify notification routing for Chassis Shield and Curb Class website lead forms. Both pipelines: webhook -> Gotify push (notification) + Twenty CRM person/opportunity sync (response). Chassis Shield two-step signup: step 1 (register) creates/updates the Person and an Opportunity marked 'Profile Incomplete' (stage: Enrichment Pending). Step 2 (enrich) finds-or-creates that Opportunity, updates it to 'Profile Enriched' (stage: Profile Enriched) with the real name, and adds an enrichment Note plus a follow-up Task (due in 24h), both linked to the Person and Opportunity. A skipped Step 2 finds-or-creates the Opportunity marked 'Skipped Profile' (stage stays Enrichment Pending) and adds a lightweight follow-up Task (no enrichment Note). Curb Class also creates a Note (+ photo attachments) and a follow-up Task on the Opportunity. Twenty API tokens are stored as flow credentials (encrypted), not plaintext env values. Partial failures in the Twenty sync chain (failed Person/Opportunity/Note/Task/link creation) are logged via node.warn for visibility without affecting the HTTP response already sent to the website. Errors anywhere in the primary pipeline are caught by the shared catch node so the originating HTTP request always gets a response.

nodes.name: Format Error Response
Validate Contact Payload
Prepare Lead Data
Process Upserted Person
Prepare Opportunity
Process Opportunity Response
Prepare Attachments
Process General Inquiry
Prepare BidAgent Request
Process Estimate Result
Patch Opportunity Stage
Format Curb Class Lead
Prepare Note
Check Note Target Link
Prepare Follow-up Task
Check Task Target Link
Handle BidAgent Error
Fallback: Gotify Alert
